2026-03-15

Beware the Ides of March, for it shall snow a fuckton, and you won’t want to do anything at all… But I’ll start the “UI components” section of the Astro blog tutorial anyway.

Hm. Looks like component names have to start with a capital letter. Not the file, just the alias used within the page. But now I have a very simple navigation for the main pages of the site working.

And now I’ve learned how to import an image. I have to import the Images module for Astro to support image handling at all, and then I have to import each image and give it an alias and alt text. All this happens in the frontmatter, so it’s used during build. It’s helpful that it requires each image to be formally imported and aliased, in case I need to reuse an image, and it saves space in release… but a little annoying if I only want a single instance of an image on a page.

So far I’ve seen that each page can import stylesheets or have its own <style> section, and each Astro component can also have its own <style>. I wonder how styling works across multiple components. I’m sure I’ll get to explore that soon.

(some time later…)

Navigation is now its own component, and the current page of the tutorial is trying to help me create a responsive hamburger meu. Instead of a hamburger menu, though, the menu is just disappearing on a narrow screen. A friend linked me to a code snippet by Kalechips, so I’ll plan to work through that.

(more time passes…)

Okay, I have something I’m at least somewhat happy with. It’s not responsive to page size, but it collapses like it should. I’ve commented out a lot of the snippet, either because it had features I didn’t especially want, or because I didn’t know what it was doing and I’d rather only run code I understand. I’ll understand at some point.

One of the advantages of this dev blog: at some point when I have more understanding, I can think, “huh, I want to rework my nav. What tutorial did I follow initially for this?” And even if I don’t have a search function running on the site, I can search through the site files for the word ‘navigation’, and I’ll find this post with the link to the original snippet. That’s why I really want this blog to exist, so that I have incentive to write down what I’m doing and why. I’m training myself to better document my work.
